key_insight: The paper builds new low-regularity finite element elasticity complexes on tetrahedral Alfeld splits that reduce both smoothness requirements and polynomial degree while ending in hybridizable symmetric stress spaces with no vertex degrees of freedom.
engineering_relevance: These elements offer a more efficient path to stable 3D solid-mechanics discretizations, especially for mixed stress-displacement formulations that benefit from hybridization and lower-order polynomial spaces.
method: Uses local Bernstein-Gelfand-Gelfand constructions on polynomial de Rham complexes over Alfeld splits.
Derives two exact local polynomial elasticity complexes: an H^2–H^1(inc) complex and a lower-regularity H^1(curl)–H(inc^+) complex.
Builds unisolvent finite elements, bubble subcomplexes, and global commuting subcomplexes for displacement, incompatibility, and stress spaces.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports proved exact sequences, dimension formulas, unisolvence, and commuting interpolation diagrams, with the lowest-order H^1(curl)–H(inc^+) case recovering the Johnson–Mercier–Křížek element.
watch_item: The construction is mathematically intricate and depends on tetrahedral Alfeld splits, so practical implementation and mesh-generation overhead may limit immediate adoption.
paper_title: Low-regularity finite element elasticity complexes with hybridizable stresses on tetrahedral Alfeld splits
